description: prepare lifecycle script automatically runs node scripts/install-git-hooks.js on npm install, silently reconfiguring git core.hooksPath without user consent
suggested_fix: Convert to explicit opt-in bin command; add CI=true guard to skip in automated environments

description: Script writes plugin files to ~/.codex/plugins/maestro/ and modifies ~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json outside repository directory
suggested_fix: Document home-directory writes in README; require explicit --allow-home-writes flag or interactive confirmation before writing

description: execSync executes git subprocesses automatically via prepare lifecycle hook, running on every npm install in any consuming repository
suggested_fix: Move git hook setup to explicit opt-in bin command; document that users must run it manually after install

description: Modifies git core.hooksPath to .githooks/ directory; if .githooks/ contents are malicious, all future git operations execute attacker code
suggested_fix: Gate with process.env.SKIP_HOOKS guard; validate .githooks/ contents and document the core.hooksPath change in developer setup docs

description: Hook command binary paths resolved from ${extensionPath} env var; attacker-controlled env var redirects hook execution to arbitrary binary
suggested_fix: Validate MAESTRO_EXTENSION_PATH is an absolute path under a known prefix at hook registration time; reject unknown path prefixes

description: MCP server binary path and working directory resolved from ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} env var; poisoned env var redirects MCP server execution
suggested_fix: Document required env var in README; add startup validation that CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT resolves to expected directory with checksum

description: devDependency c8 pinned with caret range (^10.1.3) allowing automatic minor and patch version upgrades on fresh installs
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version: "c8": "10.1.3" to ensure reproducible installs

description: MAESTRO_RUNTIME env var selects platform adapter at runtime without validation; untrusted input could load unexpected or unintended adapter
suggested_fix: Validate MAESTRO_RUNTIME against an allowlist of known adapter names before loading the adapter module

description: Frontmatter lacks model field; all 75 agents in src/agents/, plugins/maestro/src/agents/, and claude/src/agents/ share this defect universally
suggested_fix: Add model field to frontmatter for all affected agents, e.g. model: claude-sonnet-4-6

description: All 25 claude/agents/ stub agents lack an output format specification in their body; stubs defer to MCP tool get_agent but output contract is not specified
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section to stub body, or ensure the MCP tool response always includes an output format specification

description: All 25 claude/agents/ stubs have zero standalone example blocks in document body; examples are embedded in YAML description field only and not surfaced as agent-body examples
suggested_fix: Move examples from description YAML value to standalone example blocks in the agent document body

description: Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' appears 4 times (2 in commentary, 2 in body including 'appropriate level of token granularity' and 'appropriate composition pattern') plus 'necessary' in Output Contract
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate level of token granularity' with measurable thresholds; name specific composition patterns instead of 'appropriate composition pattern'

description: Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' in commentary, 'comprehensive' twice in persona and methodology sections, 'proper' in mocking guidance, 'relevant' in input type description
suggested_fix: Replace 'comprehensive test suite' with specific coverage targets; replace 'proper mocking' with concrete mocking criteria and named patterns

description: Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in commentary and body, 'proper' twice in implementation standards and error handling guidance, 'necessary' in Output Contract
suggested_fix: Replace 'proper return types' with 'explicit type annotations'; replace 'proper error handling' with 'typed error classes or Result type pattern'

description: Five vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in methodology (including 'appropriate denormalization'), 'proper' in indexing guidance, 'ensure' in query frequency check, 'necessary' in Output Contract
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate denormalization' with 'denormalization where join cost exceeds N ms'; specify concrete index criteria rather than 'proper indexing strategies'

description: No Output Format section in body; agent has Testing Standards and Test Types sections but omits any specification of how results or reports should be structured
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section describing test report structure; consider adopting the Output Contract template used by other claude/src/agents/

description: Body has Methodology, Work Areas, and Constraints sections but no Output Format section; inconsistent with most other claude/src/agents/ which include an Output Contract
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section or Output Contract template consistent with peer agents in claude/src/agents/

description: Body has Methodology, Work Areas, and Constraints sections but no Output Format section; inconsistent with most other claude/src/agents/
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section or Output Contract template consistent with peer agents

description: Body has Methodology, Work Areas, and Constraints sections but no Output Format section; inconsistent with most other claude/src/agents/
suggested_fix: Add Output Format section or Output Contract template consistent with peer agents

description: Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' in commentary, 'proper' twice in patterns and decision framework sections, 'necessary' in Output Contract boilerplate
suggested_fix: Replace 'proper patterns' with specific named patterns; replace 'proper decision frameworks' with named frameworks such as ADR or DACI

description: Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in methodology including 'appropriate to the task type' and 'appropriate interaction patterns', 'efficient' in decision table, 'necessary' in Output Contract
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate to the task type' with a concrete decision table mapping task types to specific patterns; remove 'efficient' qualifier or replace with measurable criteria

description: Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice in commentary and protocol section including 'appropriate RTS protocol', 'proper' in lock analysis, 'necessary' in Output Contract
suggested_fix: Replace 'appropriate RTS protocol' with a named protocol decision table; specify which lock types to analyze instead of 'proper lock analysis'

description: Four vague word instances: 'appropriate' twice (example body and commentary), 'proper' in example body, 'necessary' in Output Contract boilerplate
suggested_fix: Replace 'where appropriate' in example with specific EVAL applicability conditions; replace 'proper' with named best practices from IBM i coding standards

description: All 25 claude/agents/ stubs defer methodology to MCP tool 'get_agent' declared in claude/.mcp.json; if CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT is unset or MCP server unavailable, all stubs function as empty agents with no guidance
suggested_fix: Document CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT setup requirement prominently; add graceful degradation message to stub body for when MCP tool is unavailable

description: Three parallel implementations exist for the same 15 core agent roles (src/agents/ at ~89, claude/src/agents/ at ~90, claude/agents/ stubs at 75); quality diverges 14 points between full-body and stub variants
suggested_fix: Establish single source-of-truth agent definitions and generate variant forms; or document intentional quality differences between plugin variants in the repo README
